findings.title: Design and tasks disagree on the source-first checkpoint plan
findings.problem: The design's Independent Verification Plan defines four source-first checkpoints, with checkpoint 1 already covering typed contracts, sampler, interval extraction, parameter loading, CMake targets, and sampler/interval tests. The task list instead splits typed contracts/params into checkpoint-1, sampler/interval extraction into checkpoint-2, shifts later scopes by one checkpoint number, and adds a final checkpoint-5 that the design never defines. That leaves the artifact bundle with two different authoritative checkpoint maps for the same verification workflow.
findings.evidence: design.md lines 436-458 define only checkpoints 1-4 and place sampler/interval work inside checkpoint 1. tasks.md lines 60-108 define checkpoints 1-5, move sampler/interval review to checkpoint-2, and introduce checkpoint-5 for final closure.
findings.description: The docs-first bundle is not self-consistent about which verification artifact paths and scope names the source-first workflow should use.
findings.recommendation: Normalize the checkpoint plan so design.md and tasks.md describe the same checkpoint count, numbering, scope split, and artifact paths before implementation starts.

findings.title: Multiple tasks are too broad for the workflow's required task granularity
findings.problem: The repository task rules require tasks to be independently verifiable chunks and to keep individual tasks under two hours of estimated work, but several tasks bundle large multi-file, multi-concern implementation waves into one checkbox. Examples include task 5.2 spanning the whole topology-evidence feature set, task 6.3 spanning protocol and runtime service changes, task 6.4 spanning all host tooling and overlay changes, and task 6.10 spanning the entire final source-first verification run.
findings.evidence: config.yaml lines 38-41 require independently verifiable chunks and tasks under 2 hours. tasks.md lines 85-107 contain several broad tasks that cover multiple modules, tools, and verification surfaces in one step rather than decomposed checkpoints.
findings.description: As written, the task plan does not meet the STRICT workflow's task-shaping rule, which weakens progress tracking and makes checkpoint ownership ambiguous during implementation.
findings.recommendation: Split the broad tasks into smaller, independently verifiable units aligned to single module groups or single verification surfaces, while keeping the existing checkpoint guardrails.
